metadata.notes: antigravity coexists with another adapter in one cwd; each observed independently from its own transcript root. Driver lacks a working wait_turn (scaffold SEAM 2 stub)

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
route: driver-gap

## Reasoning

**Agent (yes).** An `agy` CLI session can run in the same cwd as a different coding agent (e.g. claudecode). Antigravity is a standalone native binary writing to its own brain store under `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/`, fully decoupled from another agent's process and transcript root.

**Daemon (full).** The daemon labels each adapter independently — disjoint `ProcessMatchers` (antigravity is `ExactName{agy}`; claudecode/codex match their own binaries) and disjoint transcript roots (antigravity tails `~/.gemini/antigravity{,-cli}/brain`, claudecode tails `~/.claude/projects`, codex `~/.codex/sessions`). Per-(adapter, session_id) state and metrics come from independent parsers, so a single --record daemon observing one shared cwd produces two distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working→ready arc and no cross-contamination. The antigravity side's arc derives entirely from its own transcript, exactly the adapter-agnostic two-independent-arcs assertion. This scenario asserts NO token/cost cross-checks for the antigravity row beyond correct labeling + independent state, so Antigravity's missing JSONL usage does not weaken it.

Note on Source rooting: the adapter declares the IDE store via `ExtraDirs` rather than rooting at the shared `~/.gemini` parent precisely to avoid colliding with the Gemini CLI adapter's `~/.gemini/tmp` — so antigravity and a coexisting gemini-cli also stay disjoint. Coexistence labeling is a designed property.

**Driver (gap:wait_turn).** The recipe is a single send + wait_turn (run-cell-multi.sh drives each adapter's own interactive driver concurrently against one shared cwd). The antigravity interactive driver's `wait_turn` (SEAM 2) is unimplemented — `DRIVE_ELICITS` is only `"send slash sleep"`, and `wait_turn` is a stubbed `not_implemented` arm. Without a turn-done poll the driver cannot bracket antigravity's working→ready arc, so the cell's settle phase would validate as missing. `record` must port SEAM 2 before driving. This is tooling work (driver-gap), not an observability limit.

## What record must do

Port SEAM 2 (`wait_turn`: poll antigravity's transcript.jsonl for a terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls — the parser's turn_done signal), then drive the coexist recipe via run-cell-multi.sh with antigravity + a partner adapter (e.g. claudecode) in one cwd.
caveats: daemon=full is derived from reading the adapter's disjoint ProcessMatcher + transcript roots; it is provisional until record proves the antigravity session is labeled correctly and transitions independently alongside the partner adapter in one shared cwd.
The partner adapter (claudecode in this recipe) must itself be recordable; this cell stores the antigravity transcript for the replay golden and shares the workspace events.jsonl.

sources.note: ProcessMatcher ExactName{agy}; Source FilesUnderRoot over ~/.gemini/antigravity{,-cli}/brain (IDE via ExtraDirs) — disjoint from other adapters' matchers/roots
ExtraDirs (not rooting at shared ~/.gemini) explicitly avoids colliding with the Gemini CLI adapter's ~/.gemini/tmp — coexistence is a designed property
DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'; wait_turn (SEAM 2) is a stubbed not_implemented arm — must be ported before recording

preconditions: agy AND claude CLIs on PATH and authenticated (agy via Google OAuth; claude via subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (its own --record daemon + IRRLICHT_HOME on an alternate port, production keeps running)
no other agy/claudecode session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches the antigravity and claudecode interactive drivers concurrently, both pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD
the isolated daemon observes antigravity via ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain and claudecode via ~/.claude/projects — both keyed off the real $HOME — labeling each adapter independently
this cell stores the antigravity transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha

verify: events.jsonl contains TWO distinct session_ids from two different adapters (antigravity + claudecode), both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the two adapters' rows; each carries its correct adapter label
